my_thoughts: 'Probably hundreds of times'—the ratio is devastating. 1 mistake, 100+ punishments. Teacher names it: 'is that thinking about a mistake or is it self-torture?' The question makes distinction unmissable. Thinking = once (proportional). Torture = hundreds (disproportional). Eight turns to get here: from automatic abuse to quantified recognition of torture. The teaching worked: (1) label abuse in real-time, (2) link to feeling, (3) note absence of self-defense, (4) distinguish learning from torture via quantification. Clean eight-turn arc. Student now has distinction: thinking vs torture. Can't unknow this.
accumulated_insight: T8 (Final): Student admits disproportion: 'Probably hundreds of times'—reveals 1 mistake vs 100+ self-attacks. Teacher makes distinction: 'So is that thinking about a mistake or is it self-torture?' The question lands the teaching: 1:100 ratio = torture, not learning. Eight-turn arc complete: unconscious self-attack (T1) → labeled as abuse (T1-2) → feels bad (T3-4) → breaks (T5) → rationalizes (T6) → quantifies (T7-8) → sees distinction (T8). Episode teaches: self-attack ≠ accountability. Thinking about mistake = once. Torture = hundreds of times.
visible_response: So is that thinking about a mistake or is it self-torture?
